DECLARATION OF ANDREW M. SPURCHISE IN SUPPORT OF UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC.'S SUPPLEMENTAL BRIEF IN SUPPORT OF PETITION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION AND STAY DLSE PROCEDINGS (TRANSACTION ID # 61466156) FILED BY PETITIONER UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC.
ORDER GRANTING IN PART PETITIONER'S PETITION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION AND FOR STAY OF DLSE PROCEEDINGS

LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONER UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC.'S MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION AND FOR A STAY OF DLSE PROCEEDINGS IS GRANTED IN PART. ALL DLSE PROCEEDINGS ARE STAYED PENDING THE CALIFORNIA SUPREME COURT'S DECISION IN OTO, L.L.C. V. KHO. ONCE THE DECISION IN OTO IS ISSUED, THE PARTIES SHOULD MEET AND CONFER REGARDING THE IMPACT OF THAT CASE ON THIS PETITION. IF ANY OF THE RESPONDENTS BELIEVE THAT THE CALIFORNIA SUPREME COURT'S DECISION IN OTO DOES NOT SUPPORT GRANTING THE PETITION, UBER MAY RE-NOTICE ITS PETITION FOR A HEARING AT THAT TIME. SEE MINUTES AND/OR TRANSCRIPT FOR DETAILS. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: G. GONZALES; REPORTER: GISELLE CASEY CSR#8090, EMAIL: CASEY.GISELLE@GMAIL.COM.

LAW AND MOTION, DEPT. 302, NOV-26-2019 HEARING HELD RE: PETITIONER UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC.'S PETITION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION AND FOR A STAY OF DLSE PROCEEDINGS. THE COURT ADOPTS THE TENTATIVE RULING. COMMISSIONER SU'S REQUEST IS GRANTED AND UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC.'S PETITION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION IS ORDERED OFF CALENDAR. THE LIMITED-DISCOVERY PERIOD SHALL END ON MARCH 20, 2020. UBER MAY THEN RE-CALENDAR ITS PETITION FOR HEARING. THE PARTIES MAY REQUEST SUPPLEMENTAL BRIEFING AT THAT TIME. THE PROCEEDINGS ARE OTHERWISE STAYED UNTIL FURTHER ORDER OF THIS COURT. ORDER IS SIGNED. JUDGE: RICHARD ULMER, CLERK: S. LE, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, REPORTED. =(302/RBU)

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Petition And Petition To Compel Arbitration And For A Stay Of Dlse Proceedings
Off Calendar Nov-26-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Sophia Behnia, esq. (415-276-2561) of Littler for Petitioner/Moving party.
Theresa J. Bichsel, esq. (415-486-2083) of State of California Department of Industrial Relations Labor Commissioner for Respondent/opposing party.

ARGUED re: crossing state line (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PETITIONER UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC.'s Petition To Compel Arbitration And For A Stay Of Dlse Proceedings.

The Federal Arbitration Act does not apply to "contracts of workers?engaged in foreign or interstate commerce." (9 U.S.C. 1.) Respondent Labor Commissioner Julie Su requests "limited discovery to develop facts relevant to the extent to which Uber drivers engage in interstate commerce." (See Singh v. Uber Technologies, Inc. (3rd Cir. 2019) 939 F.3d 210; Sienkaniec v. Uber Technologies, Inc. (D. Minn. 2019) No. 17-CV-4489 (PSJ/HB) 2019 WL 4308555, at *1.) Commissioner Su's request is granted and Uber Technologies, Inc.'s petition to compel arbitration is ordered off calendar. The limited-discovery period shall end on March 20, 2020. Uber may then re-calendar its petition for hearing. The parties may request supplemental briefing at that time. The proceedings are otherwise stayed until further order of this court.

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard Ulmer, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)

MOTION To Compel Arbitration And For A Stay Of Dlse Proceedings
Off Calendar Jan-04-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Andrew Spurchise, Esq. [T:212.503.2694] with Sophia Behnia, Esq. [T:415.276.2561] of Littler Mendelson, PC, for petitioner/moving party Uber Technologies Inc.;
Theresa J. Bichsel, Esq. of the Labor Commissioner's Office, T:415.486.2083 for respondent/opposing party Julie Su.

RULING: The matter was argued. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted the tentative ruling as follows:

PETITIONER UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC.'S Motion To Compel Arbitration And For A Stay Of DLSE Proceedings is GRANTED IN PART. All DLSE proceedings are stayed pending the California Supreme Court's decision in OTO, L.L.C. v. Kho. Once the decision in OTO is issued, the parties should meet and confer regarding the impact of that case on this petition. If any of the respondents believe that the California Supreme Court's decision in OTO does not support granting the petition, Uber may re-notice its petition for a hearing at that time. Due to the significant uncertainty of the applicable law in this area and the California Supreme Court's grant of review in OTO, this court believes the wisest course is not to decide the merits of the petition at this time and to wait for guidance from the California Supreme Court. This view is bolstered by the court's belief that: a) if the petition were denied, it is likely that Uber would file an appeal and the appeal would be held pending the California Supreme Court's ruling in OTO and b) if the petition were granted, it is likely that one of the respondents would seek writ review and the writ would be held pending the California Supreme Court's ruling in OTO.

ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Reporter: Giselle Casey CSR#8090, email: casey.giselle@gmail.com.

PETITIONER UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC'S MOTION To Compel Arbitration And For A Stay Of DLSE Proceedings is CONTINUED to December 19, 2017 at 2:00 PM in Department 302/Law and Motion. Uber shall file a supplemental brief no later than December 5 and limited to the issue of the new case. Respondent's opposition is due no later than December 12. No reply.
